Metadata.title: Resume Maxxer | ATS-Friendly Resume Builder
Metadata.description: Build polished resumes in a clean client-side editor with Typst-powered PDF generation, ATS-safe layouts, and fast template switching.

Footer.description: Create a professional resume that gets recruiter attention and passes ATS screening without formatting issues.

DashboardNavbar.editorSubtitle: Fill in the sections on the left to generate your PDF.
DashboardNavbar.templatesSubtitle: Choose the layout and style of your resume.
DashboardNavbar.atsSubtitle: Check resume readability and optimize the content.
DashboardNavbar.historySubtitle: Manage document versions and exported JSON source data.

Faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
Faq.subtitle: How the platform works, how data is handled, and why the Typst engine improves document quality.

items.q: Why is this resume builder better than typical graphic tools and text editors?
items.a: Traditional editors often break columns, spacing, and structure, especially when files are opened elsewhere or parsed by ATS software. Resume Maxxer generates a clean PDF that keeps the structure intact and remains machine-readable.

items.q: What is the benefit of separating resume content from presentation?
items.a: The work history is entered once. After that, the template can change freely in the gallery while the content automatically adapts to margins, hierarchy, and spacing.

items.q: Do I need to pay to download the generated PDF?
items.a: No. The builder is free to use. There is no required account, no credit card, and no forced registration for the core workflow.

items.q: How does Typst improve ATS readability?
items.a: Typst is a modern typesetting engine that produces a clean stream of text and vector instructions rather than deeply nested layout markup. That helps preserve structure for ATS and keeps the final document visually consistent.

Faq.contactTitle: Have technical questions or a new template idea?
Faq.contactDescription: Feedback from engineers, designers, and power users is always welcome.

Authors.title: About Us
Authors.subtitle: Meet the mission behind a resume builder focused on clarity, ATS safety, and practical outcomes.
Authors.manifestBody1: Most resume tools on the web still rely on decorative layouts packed with sidebars, tables, and visual tricks. They may look polished on screen, but they often become fragile in real hiring workflows where ATS systems expect clean structure and predictable text flow.
Authors.manifestBody2: Resume Maxxer was built as a practical response to that problem. The goal is simple: help people create resumes that look sharp, stay readable, and survive automated screening without sacrificing editorial quality.
Authors.founderDescription: Engineer and product builder focused on creating practical tools that help people move faster in their careers while keeping control over their private data.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.subtitle: How Resume Maxxer handles personal data, local storage, PDF generation, and optional integrations.

sections.title: 1. General provisions
paragraphs: This Privacy Policy explains how personal data is handled when using Resume Maxxer.
The core editor does not require account creation or identity verification to access the main features.

sections.title: 2. Local-first data processing
paragraphs: The application is designed with a client-side architecture. Resume content entered in the editor is stored locally in the browser using local storage.
The platform does not maintain a central database of user resumes and does not archive editor content by default.

sections.title: 3. PDF generation and API usage
paragraphs: When a PDF is generated, the resume JSON is sent temporarily to the compilation service required to render the document.
That transfer is technical and short-lived. The payload is only used to compile the file and is not meant to persist beyond the processing window.
If optional AI features are used, selected text fragments may be sent to language model providers in a stateless request flow.

sections.title: 4. Cookies and analytics
paragraphs: The platform does not rely on third-party tracking cookies, marketing pixels, or invasive profiling systems for the core experience.
Only essential browser mechanisms needed to preserve application state in a session may be used.

sections.title: 5. User rights
paragraphs: Because the main resume data is stored locally, users retain direct control over that data at all times.
bullets: Review and edit the resume directly inside the application.
Export the full data set to JSON at any time.
Delete locally stored resume data by clearing browser storage.

sections.title: 6. Policy updates
paragraphs: This policy may be updated as the product evolves or legal requirements change. The current version is always published on this page.

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.subtitle: Terms governing the electronic delivery of Resume Maxxer features.

sections.title: Section 1. General provisions
paragraphs: These Terms define the legal and technical rules for using Resume Maxxer.
The service provides tooling for creating, editing, validating, and exporting resume documents, including ATS-oriented formatting and Typst-based PDF generation.
Using the service means accepting these Terms from the first interaction with the interface.

sections.title: Section 2. Service conditions
paragraphs: Access to the core editor, live preview, ATS checks, and PDF or JSON export is available without mandatory registration.
Proper use of the service requires a modern browser with JavaScript and local storage enabled, plus internet access during PDF compilation.
The service must not be used for unlawful, harmful, or misleading document generation.

sections.title: Section 3. Intellectual property and candidate data
paragraphs: Copyright in the resume content entered by the user remains with the user.
Users retain the right to use generated PDFs and exported JSON for private, commercial, or recruiting purposes.
The application code, interface, validation logic, and templates remain the intellectual property of the service creators.

sections.title: Section 4. Disclaimer
paragraphs: The service is provided on an 'as is' basis without warranties of a specific recruiting outcome.
Every effort is made to produce ATS-friendly documents, but no guarantee can be made for every third-party hiring system or screening workflow.
The operators are not responsible for employer decisions made on the basis of generated documents.

sections.title: Section 5. Final provisions
paragraphs: The service may be temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, upgrades, or infrastructure work.
Where these Terms do not address a matter directly, applicable law governs.

Comparison.title:
Why do regular editors

make job search harder?
Comparison.description: Strong candidates often lose momentum because of broken files. See how to save time and stay confident in every application.
Comparison.traditionalSubtitle: Popular graphic builders and text editors
Comparison.maxxerSubtitle: A modern builder built to avoid formatting failures

traditionalFlaws.title: Layouts that break
traditionalFlaws.desc: Columns, spacing, and margins often shift when the file is opened on another machine.

traditionalFlaws.title: Poor ATS readability
traditionalFlaws.desc: Recruiting systems struggle with decorative templates and can miss key parts of the content.

traditionalFlaws.title: Manual layout fixes
traditionalFlaws.desc: Adding one sentence can break the page and force a round of tedious visual edits.

traditionalFlaws.title: Repeated data entry
traditionalFlaws.desc: Changing the resume design often means rebuilding the same work history from scratch.

maxxerAdvantages.title: ATS-safe output
maxxerAdvantages.desc: Clean PDF output helps applicant tracking systems read the full content of your resume.

maxxerAdvantages.title: Automatic layout control
maxxerAdvantages.desc: Spacing, hierarchy, and margins stay aligned as the content changes.

maxxerAdvantages.title: Switch designs in one click
maxxerAdvantages.desc: Enter the data once and swap templates without losing content or structure.

maxxerAdvantages.title: Tailored to the job
maxxerAdvantages.desc: The builder helps highlight the skills and signals employers expect to see.

Faq.title:
Everything you need to know about

the resume builder
Faq.description: Answers to the most common questions about building a strong resume.

items.question: Why is this builder better than regular design tools?
items.answer: Traditional tools often break layout consistency across devices and make ATS parsing less reliable. This builder produces a clean PDF that keeps formatting stable.

items.question: What is the benefit of separating content from layout?
items.answer: The work history is entered once, then the design can change without rewriting or manually rebuilding the document.

items.question: Is the builder free to use?
items.answer: Yes. The core builder is free and does not require a credit card or mandatory account creation.

Faq.moreQuestionsTitle: Have more questions about your resume?

Wizard.completedAlert: All sections are filled in. You can now review the PDF preview or check the ATS audit.

basics.title: Profile, contact, and target role
basics.description: Start with the information that sets the direction for the whole resume.
basics.checklist: Contact details, target role, job post, short summary.

experience.title: Experience and outcomes
experience.description: Separate responsibilities from results so it is easier to build strong resume bullets later.
experience.checklist: Roles, company context, responsibilities, metrics, achievements.

education.title: Education, courses, and achievements
education.description: This section matters most for students, junior candidates, and career changers.
education.checklist: Schools, fields, courses, academic projects, honors.

skills.title: Skills and keywords
skills.description: Add only concrete skills that match the role and that you can defend in conversation or through experience.
skills.checklist: Tools, systems, domain skills, certified capabilities.

projects.title: Projects, initiatives, and portfolio
projects.description: Add work that shows practical application of your skills beyond the job title itself.
projects.checklist: Project goal, role, tools, scope, outcome.

extra.title: Languages, certifications, and extras
extra.description: Add details that strengthen credibility or are explicitly required by the role.
extra.checklist: Languages, levels, licenses, certifications, proof links.

Basics.introTitle: Who are you and what role are you targeting?
Basics.introDescription: These details help tailor the resume tone, headline, and keywords to a specific hiring process.
Basics.contactTitle: How should a recruiter reach you?
Basics.contactDescription: Include only the details a recruiter needs. The resume location will be built from country, city, and optional remote availability.
Basics.summaryDescription: Write 2-4 lines in third person: years of experience, specialization, strongest achievements, and fit for the role.
Basics.linksDescription: Choose the platforms that matter. The right links differ for designers, engineers, salespeople, creators, or researchers.
Basics.empty: No links yet. Add only profiles that genuinely strengthen the application.

title.label: Current professional title
title.placeholder: e.g. Customer Support Specialist, Accountant, Electrician

targetRole.label: Target role
targetRole.placeholder: e.g. Shift Manager, Junior Analyst, Warehouse Associate

targetCompany.label: Target company / industry
targetCompany.placeholder: e.g. logistics, banking, education, specific company

targetJobDescription.label: Job description
targetJobDescription.helper: If you have the job post, paste it here. If not, leave it empty and fill in the rest of the resume normally.
targetJobDescription.placeholder: Requirements, responsibilities, role title, keywords from the posting...

remoteFriendly.label: Are you open to remote work too?
remoteFriendly.helper: If checked, the generated resume may format the location as `city / Remote`.

summary.placeholder: Customer support specialist with 4 years of experience working with business clients, complaint handling, and CRM systems...

Experience.title: Describe each role the way it should appear on the resume
Experience.description: Add company, role, dates, location, responsibilities, and concrete outcomes. Nothing else is needed here.
Experience.emptyDescription: Add full-time work, freelance work, internships, self-employment, or other domain experience. The strongest entries show impact, not just responsibilities.
Experience.tip: Fill this in top to bottom: company and role, dates, responsibilities, then concrete outcomes with numbers.

responsibilities.label: Core responsibilities
responsibilities.placeholder: e.g. Managed business customer accounts in a CRM system
responsibilities.helper: Add the most important work areas. One bullet should describe one concrete responsibility.

highlights.label: Achievements and results
highlights.placeholder: e.g. Reduced complaint handling time from 5 days to 2 business days
highlights.helper: Add 2-5 strongest outcomes. Ideally one bullet = one action and one result.

Education.title: Education can be the main proof of competence
Education.description: If you have limited experience or are changing industries, add schools, fields, and relevant courses that support the target role.
Education.emptyDescription: Add schools, studies, vocational courses, or training that support the target role.
Education.tip: Start with formal details, then add only the courses and subjects that truly support the application.

degree.label: Degree / title
degree.placeholder: Choose one or enter your own

field.label: Field / specialization
field.placeholder: Choose one or enter your own

coursework.label: Relevant courses and subjects
coursework.helper: Add only items that genuinely support the role you are applying for.

Skills.title: Add only skills you can defend
Skills.description: Choose a category structure that fits your field. The template only displays the data, while the preset helps you organize it sensibly.
Skills.suggestedDescription: Click a category to add skills there right away or create an empty group.
Skills.emptyDescription: Add 6-12 of the most relevant skills for the target role and job post.

general.label: General
general.description: Good for most business, operations, administrative, and service roles.
general.placeholder: e.g. Salesforce, Excel, complaint handling, AutoCAD, HACCP

engineering.label: IT / Engineering
engineering.description: A split designed for technical resumes: languages, frameworks, testing, data, DevOps, and tooling.
engineering.placeholder: e.g. Python, TypeScript, FastAPI, React, Pytest, Ruff, PostgreSQL, Docker

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: Full freedom. Build your own groups exactly around your field and your resume narrative.
custom.placeholder: e.g. enter your own category and add concrete skills to it

Projects.title: Add projects that show practical skills
Projects.description: These can be work projects, school projects, personal work, volunteer efforts, or process improvements. Your role and the outcome matter most.
Projects.emptyDescription: Add work projects, academic work, portfolio pieces, process improvements, or meaningful side initiatives.
Projects.tip: This entry should answer four questions: what it was, what your role was, what tools were used, and what the result was.

name.label: Project / initiative name
name.placeholder: e.g. CRM rollout, warehouse audit, sales campaign

url.label: Link / online asset
url.placeholder: e.g. portfolio, publication, repository, case study

organization.label: Organization / context
organization.placeholder: e.g. university, company, client, personal project

keywords.label: Tools / methods / keywords (comma-separated)
keywords.placeholder: e.g. Excel, CRM, data analysis, Figma, AutoCAD, Lean, SQL

description.label: Project goal and scope
description.placeholder: Describe why the project existed, what problem it solved, who it served, and what your responsibility was.

highlights.label: Project outcomes
highlights.placeholder: e.g. Reduced reporting time from 3 hours to 30 minutes
highlights.helper: Add the most important project results. Each bullet should describe what improved or what was delivered.

Extra.title: Add only information that strengthens the application
Extra.description: Languages, licenses, and certifications are strongest when they matter for the role or confirm a concrete skill.

tips.languages: Use standard language levels without stars or progress bars.
tips.certifications: Add certifications with issuer, date, and validity status.

languages.title: Languages
languages.emptyDescription: Add only languages that matter for the role or that you can genuinely use professionally.

certifications.title: Certifications, licenses, and awards
certifications.emptyDescription: Add certifications, licenses, training, and awards relevant to the target role.

name.label: Certification / award name
name.placeholder: e.g. AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate

status.label: Status
status.helper: Most certifications are permanent. The expiry date is shown only for time-limited certifications.

details.label: Scope / confirmed skills (optional)
details.placeholder: e.g. process auditing, accounting basics, project management, operation of a specific system

TemplateGallery.title: Template Gallery
TemplateGallery.description: Choose the layout that fits your profession best. Every template is designed to stay readable and highlight your strengths clearly.
TemplateGallery.comingSoonAlert: This template will be available in an upcoming library update.
TemplateGallery.emptyDescription: Try a different query or choose another category.

atlas-classic.description: The most formal active template: single-column, calm, and conservative, with simple section lines and a layout optimized for ATS readability.

meridian-clean.description: A clean sans-serif layout with light section rhythm, structured entries, and a more contemporary tone than classic resume layouts.

summit-serif.description: A serif template with an engineering feel: centered header, strong section hierarchy, and a slightly more academic tone without losing business readability.

contour-timeline.description: The most academic active template: wide margins, calm serif typography, and sections arranged like a well-ordered timeline.

vector-compact.description: A dense technical one-pager designed to maximize space: minimal margins, tight rhythm, and fast experience scanning.

horizon-sidebar.description: The most expressive template in the catalog: colorful sidebar, skill tags, and a modern split between primary and supporting content.

harbor-serif.description: An elegant, slightly editorial serif layout with left-side section labels. Works well when the resume should feel more organized than technical.

beacon-technical.description: A planned family for a technical resume with stronger emphasis on ownership, architecture, and projects. Not available in the app yet.

north-executive.description: A planned family for executive and leadership roles with more emphasis on responsibility scope, results, and business impact. Not available yet.

strata-academic.description: A planned family for a full academic CV with publications, grants, and research work. Not available in the current catalog yet.

delta-editorial.description: A planned family for a more magazine-like, visually bold resume. The direction is defined, but the template itself is not implemented yet.
